seoTitle: Newtown vs Nichols Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Newtown and Nichols Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Newtown vs Nichols Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Newtown and Nichols Point.
heading: Newtown vs Nichols Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Newtown, Victoria and Nichols Point, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Nichols Point has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Newtown (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Newtown or Nichols Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Newtown has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Nichols Point scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Newtown scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Nichols Point scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Newtown or Nichols Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Nichols Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
